business.headline: 8th Pay Commission timeline out: Here's what employees can expect nextIf you've been waiting for clarity on the 8th Pay Commission, there's finally some movement. The government has shared a timeline, giving employees a better idea of when changes in salaries and pensions could come.

Thinking of buying property with a credit card? Read this firstExperts say buying property abroad falls under capital account transactions, which come with strict rules. Credit cards are meant for daily expenses like travel or shopping, not for large investments like real estate.
Sensex ends 1,200 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; Titan up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1205.00 points to end at 75,273.45, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 394.05 points to close at 23,306.45.

Can mutual funds be gifted? All you need to know about Sebi's PPI planThe idea is simple. A person can purchase a Gift PPI using bank transfer or UPI. This prepaid card can then be gifted to another person. The recipient can redeem the card on an asset management company's platform and invest the amount in mutual funds.

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver rises Rs 14,000: Smart buying opportunity or risk?Gold and silver have made a strong comeback, drawing fresh attention from investors. But with prices rising so quickly, is this a good time to buy or a moment to pause and reassess?

41% borrowers are Gen Z: Is hidden debt rising among young Indians?A growing number of young Indians are turning to credit early in life. With Gen Z now making up 41% of borrowers, is this a sign of financial confidence or a warning of rising hidden debt? Let's find out.

LPG booking timelines unchanged; Centre assures steady supplyAuthorities have urged people not to believe or share unverified information. They have also advised against panic booking of cylinders, stressing that there is enough supply available across the country.

8th Pay Commission: Can government salaries finally match private sector perks?For years, government employees have measured their pay against private sector packages. Now, with the 8th Pay Commission seeking feedback, will it lead to a more balanced pay structure? Let's have a look.

Sensex jumps 1,300 points: Why is stock market rising today despite Iran war?Sensex, Nifty jump: The main trigger for today's rally is growing hope that tensions in the Iran conflict may ease. Reports suggest that the United States is working on a possible month-long ceasefire and has shared a 15-point plan with Iran.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets rise as crude oil prices fall?Early signals suggest that investors may return to equities after a volatile phase driven by war concerns and rising energy costs. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,095 as of 8:03 am, indicating that the Nifty may open above Tuesday's close of 22,912.40.
Sensex, Nifty gain over hopes of de-escalation in West Asia WarSensex jumps 700 points, Nifty above 23,100; SBI up 2%

West Asia war brings Indian aviation to its kneesA spike in global crude prices, closure of busy aviation hubs, and sweeping airspace restrictions across West Asia have collectively delivered a body blow to the airline industry that no carrier was prepared for.

Not just $580 million oil trades, huge S&P bets were placed just before Trump postWhat began with unusually timed oil trades worth around $580 million has now widened, with massive bets in S&P 500 futures also coming under scrutiny, even as the White House has dismissed the allegations.

Bangladesh hikes jet fuel prices by nearly 80% amid global energy crisisThe Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) on Tuesday increased jet fuel prices by nearly 80 percent, marking the second revision this month. The move comes as the ripple effect of rising global fuel rates, as tensions in key oil-producing regions continue to disrupt market stability.
Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil after US waiver, sources sayThe development comes after the US administration announced a 30-day waiver on certain sanctions related to Iranian oil.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has suggested that India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Only 40% women invest beyond FDs and gold: Why the gap still exists?Indian women are known for their strong saving habits, but when it comes to investing beyond fixed deposits and gold, many still hold back. What is stopping them from taking that next step?
Markets bounce back, Sensex up 1,300 points, banks leadThe Sensex surged 1,372 points to close at 74,068.45, while the Nifty 50 rose 399.75 points to settle at 22,912.40, reclaiming the 22,900 mark.

Markets in panic mode, gold and silver also hit: What's left for investors?Since the Iran war began, the Sensex has fallen about 10.6%, while the Nifty has dropped nearly 11%. At the same time, gold and silver have also declined, leaving investors with limited places to hide.

Form 16 gone from April 2026: What will your employer give you instead?A key document for salaried taxpayers is set to change. Starting April 2026, Form 16 may no longer be issued, with a new format stepping in to reshape how your income details are reported.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 unstoppable overseas with Rs 250 crore haul, never-seen-before buzz Dhurandhar 2 continues its global rampage, delivering record-breaking numbers across overseas markets. Here's how the Ranveer Singh starrer is rewriting box office history in just days.

Jaadu x Rocky: Jealous of Hrithik Roshan, Ryan Gosling pitches a wild crossover Hollywood star Ryan Gosling praised Hrithik Roshan and his iconic alien character Jaadu from Koi...Mil Gaya. During promotion of his upcoming film Project Hail Mary, he also hinted at a potential crossover.

Nivin Pauly reacts to comparisons with Mohanlal ahead of Prathichaya release Nivin Pauly has dismissed comparisons with Mohanlal, opening up about his laid-back and instinctive approach to acting ahead of the release of his upcoming film Prathichaya on March 26.

Inside pics from SS Rajamouli's grand Varanasi sets in Hyderabad Rajamouli recreated Varanasi in Hyderabad for Mahesh Babu-Priyanka Chopra's epic adventure film. Pictures from the sets show miniature sets of Varanasi based on which the extravagant sets are being built.

All about Harshil Kalia, 30-year-old actor-model who died in Jaipur crash The death of 30-year-old actor-model Harshil Kalia in a late-night car crash has shocked the entertainment industry. A rising face in regional cinema, her promising career was cut short.

BTS' 18.4 million-strong comeback: Why the record-breaking spree has just begun BTS The Comeback Live: ARIRANG drew in a staggering global viewership of 18.4 million on Netflix, surpassing the records of major awards shows across streaming, including that of the Grammys (14.4M) and VMAs (5.5M), and more.

He's in film, not in theatre: The auto driver who can't afford to watch Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is smashing box office records, yet the auto-rickshaw driver who appeared briefly in the film hasn't been able to watch it in theatres due to financial constraints.

Dhurandhar 2's terrifying bathroom scene, and what Aditya Dhar told Pinda Actor Udaybir Sandhu, opened up on Aditya Dhar's feedback on his terrifying bathroom scene in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Udaybir portrayed Ranveer Singh's estranged friend Pinda in the espionage thriller.

Dhurandhar 2 effect: Canada theatre adds rare 15-minute-long interval, fans react Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has brought back the intermission in Canadian theatres after nearly 20 years, thanks to its long runtime and massive buzz. The film is dominating the domestic and global box offices.

The Rasputin story that changes how you see that one Madhavan scene in Dhurandhar 2 A closer look at how Aditya Dhar uses Rasputin in Dhurandhar: The Revenge to transform a viral scene into layered storytelling - from the folklore of Grigori Rasputin to the cinematic unravelling of power and betrayal.

Mukesh Chhabra breaks down Dhurandhar casting: 300 roles in two years Casting director Mukesh Chhabra opened up about the two-year, 300-role casting marathon for Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He also talked about director Aditya Dhar's meticulous vision that made it all possible.

Dragon director Ashwath Marimuthu: I almost directed Rajinikanth's Thalaivar 173 Ashwath Marimuthu opens up on developing a script for Rajinikanth, nearly directing Thalaivar 173 before the project moved to Cibi Chakravarthy.

Ram Charan injured on Peddi set, returns to shoot after stitches Ram Charan sustained a minor injury above his eye while shooting an action sequence for his film Peddi. The incident, clarified by his team, caused brief concern among fans but did not halt production.

Anurag Dobhal fears he may not walk again after car crash: Leg is numb Bigg Boss 17 fame Anurag Dobhal has shared a distressing health update after a severe car crash, revealing one of his legs is completely numb. He fears he may never walk again.

The most haunting scene in Dhurandhar 2 that says everything without saying much Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2 is high on testosterone - it hits more than it says. Yet, its most haunting, cathartic moment comes without any gore. Here's breaking down the scene with the potential of leaving you speechless, wondering and taking a leaf out of Jassi's emotional playbook.

Grey's Anatomy's Caterina Scorsone-Fightmaster spark dating buzz, walk hand-in-hand Grey's Anatomy co-stars Caterina Scorsone and ER Fightmaster have sparked dating rumours after being seen together in Los Angeles, hinting at an off-screen romance. They played each other's love interest in Season 19 of the show.

Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla declared family-friendly, gets UA 16 rating  Actor Akshay Kumar's horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla secured UA 16 certificate. The CBFC advised parental guidance for the Priyadarshan directorial, particularly for viewers under 16, owing to its spooky themes.

Punjab doctor who treated Arjun Rampal lands surprise role in Dhurandhar 2 A Punjab doctor who treated Arjun Rampal on the Dhurandhar: The Revenge set landed a surprise cameo alongside Ranveer Singh. Here's how it happened.

Dhurandhar to KGF Chapter 2: Sanjay Dutt scores 3 Rs 1,000 crore hits Sanjay Dutt has remarkably delivered three consecutive Rs 1,000 crore hits. His powerful performances in key supporting and antagonist roles continue to captivate audiences and command massive box office success.

general news.headline: Iran says it fired missile at USS Abraham Lincoln an hour after warningIran has claimed it fired a cruise missile towards the USS Abraham Lincoln after issuing warnings to the United States, though there is no independent confirmation or US response.

Shutdown of US bases, Hormuz transit fees: Iran sets tough terms for ceasefireFrom shutting US bases to controlling a key global oil route, Iran's demands in response to Trump's proposal set a steep stage for talks, but hints of flexibility quietly emerge behind the scenes.

Why did Trump call Modi after 4 weeks of war? There may be a Pakistan angleUS President Donald Trump dialled Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday, four weeks after the Iran war started. The timing is crucial, coming on the back of Pakistan's emergence as Trump's choice as a mediator. Was Trump's call a damage-control exercise?

Twice bitten, thrice shy: Why Iran is wary of Trump's peace talks pushDonald Trump has said that the US was engaged in productive talks with Iran to end the war. Iran has, however, rejected Trump's claims publicly. Tehran has already been burnt twice in the name of negotiations over its nuclear program.

Dhurandhar 2 hangover: 5 unanswered questions that still hit hardAditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has wreaked havoc at the box office and has largely received a positive response from critics and audiences. However, a few questions remain unanswered about some of the supporting characters in the film.

Pakistan hands over Trump's 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran as war rages onAccording to officials in Islamabad, the proposal was handed over through Pakistani intermediaries, underscoring Islamabad's emerging role as a potential mediator between the two adversaries.

Give us 0.5%: Netherlands cricketer's blunt take on Rs 16,660 crore RCB saleAfter RCB's Rs. 16,660 crore sale to a multi-industry consortium, Netherlands pacer Paul van Meekeren said that 0.5 percent of that price is enough to change Dutch cricket and help them bridge the gap to big teams in international cricket.
India pledges 45% emission cut, targets 60% clean power by 2035New targets, old track record. India has stepped up on the global climate stage with ambition.

Creatine is the top-selling supplement, what makes it so popular?Many of us take supplements, and we are always concerned about their safety. This series of our stories will address all your concerns. This is the first of the series on supplements which covers every aspect of creatine.

Live: Iran's President praises Turkey's firm stance against IsraelUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On the fourth week of the US-Israel-Iran war, Washington sent a 15-point truce plan to Tehran to end the Middle East conflict. The conditions include strict curbs on Tehran's nuclear programme, an end to support for proxy groups and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Washington is also pushing for a month-long ceasefire to enable negotiations. However, with Iran's military dismissing Trump's claim of negotiation talks, it seems unlikely that Iran will accept a proposal compromising on its nuclear facilities. 

Meanwhile, both Iran and Israel continue to attack each other on Wednesday. Israel targeted several places in Tehran with newly launched air raids on Wednesday. Iran, on the other hand, targeted US and Israeli military bases in the Gulf region in retaliatory measures. Catch all the live updates of the prevailing Middle East conflict on Indiatoday.in-

Joke of the year: Shehbaz Sharif's mediation offer and Trump's retweetPakistan, a country struggling to contain violence within and around its own borders, now wants to broker peace between the US and Iran. The contradiction is glaring.
More rain soon: Western disturbances to hit India. See forecastJust as North India was preparing to begin summer, the weather is bound to shift again.

Didn't know he's a billionaire: Man who rescued Gautam Singhania after boat crash"At that time, we did not know that one of them was an Indian billionaire," a Maldives official said after rushing to aid victims of a speedboat crash involving industrialist Gautam Singhania.

Tarique Rahman's genocide message signals shift away from Pakistan?Tarique Rahman invoked the 1971 genocide at Pakistan's hands, countering what critics described as the soft-pedalling of those atrocities under the Muhammad Yunus-led caretaker government. The message signals Bangladesh's intent to keep Pakistan at arm's length while projecting a balanced regional posture.

Get piped gas if available or risk losing LPG supply: GovernmentA looming gas crunch and a new rule could change how millions cook, with a strict deadline now in place, households may soon face a choice that is not really optional.

Bombay Shaving Company intern does pull-ups at work; CEO's blown away, you'll be tooShantanu Deshpande recorded the moment from inside the workplace, spotlighting Akash Sharma, a supply chain intern, who casually broke into a set of pull-ups in the middle of a regular workday.

Techie works 3 hours, earns Rs 40 LPA; parents fear something is offA 24-year-old earning Rs 40 LPA should be a dream come true, but for one family, it's become a reason to worry. His 2-3 hour workdays are raising eyebrows at home, sparking a larger question: does success still need to look like struggle?

Dhurandhar 2 box office collection live: Ranveer, Arjun, Rakesh Bedi on filmDhurandhar The Revenge live updates: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is on a phenomenal run at the box office – both domestic and worldwide. The film has finished six days at the ticket window and is looking at recording the biggest Wednesday for a Hindi film.

Trump's only option in the Iran war now is a nightmareTrump's pausing strikes against Iran for negotiations is being seen as the second betrayal by his voter base after a "peace president" waging war abroad. Trump can't afford to project American weakness. The five days end when Saturday begins, and it's going to get far worse from here on.

Rs 18 crore toll, Iran nod: Why crossing Strait of Hormuz is not so straightThe Strait of Hormuz has long been considered one of the most strategically sensitive bottlenecks. Amid the Iran war, shipping has been squeezed to a trickle. How many ships are struck? Which countries have been allowed passage? Is Iran charging a toll? We have divided the article into sections. Read on to find out.

Crude oil prices have surged over $100 a barrel. With the energy infrastructure in the Middle East likely to take years to be rebuilt, the world could be set for the biggest oil disruption in history.

Isro reveals the cost of first module of Bharatiya Antariksh StationThe overall configuration of the Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) has already been reviewed by a national-level committee, marking a key milestone in the country's human spaceflight ambitions.

India's first patient recovery guidelines: All you need to knowA new set of national guidelines aims to reshape how doctors approach recovery – placing vitamins and minerals at the centre of the journey.

sports.headline: Villain Gautam Gambhir doesn't worry about being liked by anyone: Ex-CSK star IPL 2026: Former CSK star Faf du Plessis and ex-India opener Aakash Chopra praised Gautam Gambhir's leadership and weighed in on whether Kolkata Knight Riders can thrive without their former mentor.

No stupid videos: Jason Gillespie makes his intent clear on Pakistan return in PSL Jason Gillespie's return to Pakistan cricket ahead of PSL 2026 has created a buzz, with the former Pakistan red-ball coach going viral for bluntly refusing to film a "stupid" arrival video for Hyderabad Kingsmen.

Incredible Amelia Kerr joins Rohit Sharma in elite club with 2nd T20I century NZW vs SAW: Amelia Kerr continued her incredible run as captain after scoring a 52-ball century in the fifth and final T20I against South Africa at the Hagley Oval in Christchurch.

Injury or routine visit? Why Jasprit Bumrah is at BCCI COE before IPL IPL 2026: Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah has not been part of the Mumbai Indians training camp. He checked into the BCCI Centre for Excellence in Bengaluru, but speculation about a fresh injury has now been debunked.

Gautam Gambhir gets personality rights protection in deepfake case The Delhi High Court has granted Gautam Gambhir protection of his personality rights and ordered the removal of objectionable deepfake and misleading content circulating across social media and internet platforms.

5th T20I: Esterhuizen, bowlers power South Africa to 3–2 series win vs New Zealand South Africa secured their first away T20I series under Shukri Conrad and a second successive bilateral series with a convincing performance against New Zealand in Christchurch.

Ben Duckett's withdrawal raises concerns over English players' future in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Ben Duckett's withdrawal from the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League has sparked fresh concerns over the reliability of English players in the league, with former India opener Aakash Chopra warning that franchises may rethink investing in young cricketers from England.

Dark chocolate for dark boy: L Sivaramakrishnan says he faced racism from teammates Laxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed how teenage encounters with racism and systemic bias shattered his confidence, offering a haunting new perspective on why one of India's greatest bowling prodigies saw his career unravel.

Livingstone still up for England selection despite explosive interview: Rob Key Rob Key said that Liam Livingstone is still under consideration for England selection despite his public outburst against the board and the current team management. Livingstone has been out of the England setup since 2025.

How can Rishabh Pant find consistency as a T20 player? Faf du Plessis decodes Faf du Plessis has said that Rishabh Pant needs to be a high-impact player to find consistency as a T20 player. The LSG captain has impressed in Tests for India but hasn't found the same vein of form in white-ball cricket.

Real Madrid injury mismanagement: Mbappe played with torn knee after wrong scan Real Madrid have come under intense scrutiny after reports of a serious medical error involving Kylian Mbappe, who was allegedly allowed to play with a knee injury following a diagnostic mix-up.

Can a reimagined Rajasthan Royals get it right under Riyan Parag?IPL 2026 preview: Rajasthan Royals head into the new season amid buzz of an ownership change and with Riyan Parag as their new captain. But the big question remains: can they finally end their long wait for glory?

Rajasthan Royals: The $67 million IPL shadow that became a $1.63 billion empireStarting as one of the low-key sides in IPL, Rajasthan Royals finds itself as an empire now after its USD 1.63 billion sale. From winning the inaugural IPL title under Shane Warne to being filled with controversies, here's a look at the Jaipur-based franchise.

Griezmann joins ex-Barcelona teammate Messi in MLS, signs for Orlando CityFrench forward Antoine Griezmann is set to join MLS club Orlando City from Atletico Madrid in 2026. Griezmann will reunite with former Barcelona teammate and Inter Miami superstar Lionel Messi in the league.

IPL Impact Player rule to stay, says Sourav Ganguly after Axar Patel's criticismIPL 2026: All-rounders have openly expressed their disdain for it, but former India captain Sourav Ganguly on Tuesday said the Indian Premier League's controversial Impact Player rule is here to stay.

Most of ECB hierarchy should be sacked: Boycott slams England's Ashes reviewFormer England captain and legendary batter Geoffrey Boycott slammed ECB chief executive Richard Gould and managing director Rob Key over the review into England's Ashes defeat earlier in the summer.

A champion at last, Virat Kohli now walks into IPL 2026 without chainsIPL 2026: The wait is over, and so is the weight that followed Virat Kohli through every IPL season. Now, with nothing left to prove and only milestones ahead, IPL 2026 could unleash his most dangerous, unrestrained version yet.

Give us 0.5%: Netherlands cricketer's blunt take on Rs 16,660 crore RCB saleAfter RCB's Rs. 16,660 crore sale to a multi-industry consortium, Netherlands pacer Paul van Meekeren said that 0.5 percent of that price is enough to change Dutch cricket and help them bridge the gap to big teams in international cricket.

Salah to leave Liverpool at the end of the season, ends 9-year stint at AnfieldMohamed Salah will leave Liverpool at the end of the season, the club confirmed on Tuesday, bringing the curtain down on one of the most decorated spells in the club's history.

IPL champions RCB sold to Aditya Birla Group-led consortium for Rs 16,660 croreIPL franchise, Royal Challengers Bengaluru, has been sold to a consortium, led by Aditya Birla Group, for a record sum of over Rs 16,000 crore, marking the league's most expensive franchise takeover ever.

Andre Russell emotional after KKR retire his No. 12 jersey in special ceremonyIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday retired Andre Russell's No. 12 jersey in honour of his contributions to the team. Russell, who retired from IPL last year, joined the backroom staff for the upcoming season, starting March 28.

When you think of KKR, it's Shah Rukh Khan first and then Rinku Singh: Ajinkya RahaneIPL 2026: Rinku Singh named KKR vice-captain as Rahane and Nayar praised his growth, leadership and impact, with the T20 World Cup winner now taking on a bigger role.

Chess great Koneru Humpy slammed for pulling out of Candidates over security fearsCyprus Chess Federation chief Criton Tornaritis has criticised Koneru Humpy's withdrawal from the Women's Candidates over safety concerns, calling it a bad move that harms the sport and organisers.

KKR announce new vice-captain, Rinku Singh to be Ajinkya Rahane's deputyIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday announced Rinku Singh as their vice-captain for the new season. Ajinkya Rahane will continue to lead the three-time champions, who finished eighth in the 10-team league last season.

RCB vs SRH tickets sell out in a minute, fans allege lack of transparencyTickets for the IPL 2026 opener between RCB and SRH sold out within minutes, leaving fans frustrated, with many alleging lack of transparency and irregularities in the booking process.

Ben Duckett pulls out of IPL 2026, apologises to Delhi Capitals, faces 2-year banIPL 2026: Ben Duckett withdraws from IPL 2026 to focus on England duty, apologises to Delhi Capitals, and could face a ban under league rules for pulling out after being picked at the auction.

IPL as big as EPL and NBA, says Sourav Ganguly after Rajasthan Royals' takeover buzzIPL 2026: Sourav Ganguly says the IPL now rivals global giants like the EPL and NBA, as soaring valuations and rising global investment underline its rapid growth and commercial dominance.

After quitting PSL, Australia pacer joins CSK as Nathan Ellis's replacementIPL 2026: Australia's Spencer Johnson became the third player to quit Pakistan Super League and join the IPL as a replacement player. Chennai Super Kings signed the left-arm fast bowler for Rs 1.5 crore.

Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu to lead India's Thomas and Uber Cup campaigns in MayWhile Lakshya Sen will lead India's campaign in Thomas Cup, two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu will return to action to lead the Uber Cup team in Horsens, Denmark, from April 24 to May 3.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
